description:
## Background
The App subscribes to the native `workspace-transition` event inside a direct React `useEffect`. The subscription is an external-system lifecycle and must comply with the no-use-effect rule without leaking listeners or applying events after disposal.

## What to build
Replace the App effect that asynchronously registers the Workspace transition listener and routes each native payload through the existing transition application logic.

## Technical Approach
Use an explicit mount/lifecycle abstraction or a keyed component boundary that supports the asynchronous listener registration. Preserve the disposed guard for a listener that resolves after unmount, store and invoke the returned unlisten function, and keep transition payload handling on the existing callback path.

## Considerations
The native listener registration is asynchronous: unmount may occur before registration completes. Cleanup must handle both cases, and a late registration must be immediately removed rather than retained. Do not change Workspace transition generation semantics.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Workspace transition events still update Issue Explorer and Workspace state exactly as before.
- [ ] A listener registered after unmount is immediately unregistered, and an existing listener is removed on cleanup.
- [ ] This subscription has no direct `useEffect` call and passes the configured Oxlint restriction.
- [ ] Focused App/event tests and relevant lint/typecheck checks pass.

## Test Plan
- Run existing App workspace transition tests.
- Add regression coverage for unmount-before-listener-resolution and cleanup if absent.
- Run the relevant Oxlint and TypeScript checks.

Triage: ready-for-agent.
title: Replace App workspace transition subscription effect
